task_context.actionable_question: Does submitting late actually lower my score?
task_context.ai_prompt_hint: Use submission_delay_avg [FE] and punctuality_rate [FE]. Explain how delay magnitude correlates with score drop.

explanation.raw_text:
Summary: You've shown a pattern of submitting assessments late, which seems to correlate with a drop in your scores. While you have passed all assessments, the increase in submission delays may be impacting your overall performance.

Insights: Increasing Submission Delays: Your submission delays have increased from 3 days to 5 days over the assessments, which is concerning. This trend suggests that you may be struggling to meet deadlines, which could affect your scores. | Score Drop with Delays: Your normalized scores have decreased from 100 to 83 as submission delays increased. This indicates a potential link between late submissions and lower scores.

Educational implications: Timely submissions may enhance your understanding and retention of course material.

Recommendations: high - Create a study schedule to manage your time better. - This will help you stay on track with your assignments and reduce submission delays. | medium - Set reminders for due dates. - Reminders can help you submit your work on time, potentially improving your scores.

required_core_outputs.description: State average submission delay and punctuality rate.
Describe the observed relationship between delay magnitude and score.

Frame the delay-score relationship as correlational, not causal.
